findings.title: Pipeline Consolidation Summary
findings.description: Consolidated 39 findings from 7 agents into 28 unique findings. Removed 11 duplicates. Identified 4 cross-agent conflicts.

findings.title: Top Priority Findings
findings.description: Highest priority items based on weighted scoring (user_impact×0.35 + business_value×0.30) / (effort×0.20 + risk×0.15): PushFlow's Strong Delivery Metrics (score=4.042); North Star Metric: User Engagement (score=3.727); Conduct A/B Testing on Notification Timing (score=2.958); Need for Notification Priority Levels (score=2.882); Add Notification Priority Levels (score=2.853)

top_priorities.title: Conduct A/B Testing on Notification Timing

findings.title: Cross-Agent Conflict Resolution Report
findings.description: Resolved 4 conflicts between agent findings. intake-001 vs requirements-004: Privacy risk detected in notification center, while accessibility improvements may require data collection that could violate privacy policies.; intake-002 vs competitive-004: Pricing concerns are flagged as high risk, while recommendations suggest evaluating pricing strategy, which could exacerbate the risk if not handled carefully.; feasibility-002 vs requirements-005: Push notifications failure on Android 14 is a critical risk, while fixing this issue is also a high-priority requirement.

evidence.excerpt: Privacy risk detected in notification center, while accessibility improvements may require data collection that could violate privacy policies.
Pricing concerns are flagged as high risk, while recommendations suggest evaluating pricing strategy, which could exacerbate the risk if not handled carefully.
Push notifications failure on Android 14 is a critical risk, while fixing this issue is also a high-priority requirement.
A gap in measuring notification delivery latency is identified, while a requirement to reduce latency is also present, potentially leading to conflicting priorities.

conflicts.description: Privacy risk detected in notification center, while accessibility improvements may require data collection that could violate privacy policies.
conflicts.resolution: Ensure that any data collected for accessibility improvements is anonymized and complies with privacy regulations.
conflicts.reasoning: Accessibility enhancements are essential, but they must not compromise user privacy. Implementing anonymization techniques can help mitigate privacy risks.

conflicts.description: Pricing concerns are flagged as high risk, while recommendations suggest evaluating pricing strategy, which could exacerbate the risk if not handled carefully.
conflicts.resolution: Conduct a thorough market analysis to adjust pricing without compromising profitability or user perception.
conflicts.reasoning: Addressing pricing concerns is critical, but it must be done strategically to avoid further risks associated with pricing adjustments.

conflicts.description: Push notifications failure on Android 14 is a critical risk, while fixing this issue is also a high-priority requirement.
conflicts.resolution: Prioritize the resolution of push notification failures on Android 14 to ensure reliable delivery before implementing additional features.
conflicts.reasoning: Resolving critical delivery issues is paramount to maintaining user engagement and trust, and must take precedence over other requirements.

conflicts.description: A gap in measuring notification delivery latency is identified, while a requirement to reduce latency is also present, potentially leading to conflicting priorities.
conflicts.resolution: First, establish a detailed tracking system for notification delivery latency, then implement optimizations based on the gathered data.
conflicts.reasoning: Understanding current latency metrics is essential before making changes, ensuring that optimizations are data-driven and effective.

summary: Consolidated 39 findings into 28 unique items. Removed 11 duplicates, resolved 4 conflicts. Top priority: PushFlow's Strong Delivery Metrics.
